div.div: Chapter . NXTCG v 0.0.1 Release Notes: What’s New and What’s Fixed

div.class: - map/map bookmap/bookmap map bookmap brand or business unit
div.domains: (map mapgroup-d) (map bookmap) (topic abbrev-d) (topic delay-d) a(props deliveryTarget) (map ditavalref-d) (topic hazard-d) (topic hi-d) (topic indexing-d) (topic markup-d) (topic pr-d) (topic relmgmt-d) (topic sw-d) (topic ui-d) (topic ut-d) (topic xnal-d) (topic markup-d xml-d)

h1.span: NXTCG v 0.0.1 Release Notes: What’s New and What’s Fixed

section.p: This release of the NXTCG platform introduces core marketplace functionality, allowing users to buy, sell, and manage NextGen trading cards through a fully integrated frontend and backend experience. The release focuses on establishing the end-to-end purchase flow, product configuration, user profile management, and platform stability.

NXTCG now supports a complete trading card marketplace experience. Users can browse available cards, configure product attributes, add customized items to their cart, and securely complete checkout. Sellers can list cards for sale and manage their inventory directly from their user profile. The platform also introduces a modern storefront interface, navigation components, and landing pages to support discovery and usability.

tr.td: Users can configure products, add them to an order, and enter payment and address details during checkout.
Users can select product quantities before adding items to their cart.
Support for configurable product attributes for trading cards.
Users can add multiple customized products to their cart.
Users can enter a delivery address during checkout.
Users can enter a billing address as part of the checkout process.
Users can submit and complete orders through the checkout workflow.
Order history now displays purchased trading cards correctly.
Introduced a landing page to support storefront access and discovery.
Implemented storefront and product views for browsing available cards.
Added navigation bar and footer views to improve site navigation.
User profiles now display the trading cards a user has listed for sale.
Sellers can delete items they have listed for sale.
Fixed an issue where the storefront displayed incorrect available listings.
Resolved an issue where order history did not return purchased cards.
Prevented users from updating their profile information and password at the same time.
User authentication tokens are now invalidated after password changes, requiring re-login.
Stabilized the automated testing environment to prevent pipeline failures.
Implemented a DB2 database connection prototype for backend services.
Created database access objects to standardize data operations.
Set up REST API endpoints to support frontend integration.
Established an automated testing environment for continuous integration.
